description.p: BedrockOnLinux installs and runs the Windows (GDK) edition of Minecraft Bedrock on Linux. One app does everything automatically:

ul.li: downloads the Minecraft version you pick;
applies the binary patches the game needs to start and to join online Bedrock servers;
fixes curl/SSL, then launches the game.

description.p: Sign in, open Play • Servers, and join native Bedrock servers (Hive, CubeCraft, …) or crossplay/Geyser servers. No game files are shipped; you supply your own — you must own Minecraft.

screenshot.caption: The launcher: pick a version, sign in, play

ul.li: the version picker now defaults to the version you last played (or the latest) instead of resetting to a different one;
the in-game sign-in and native-server-join binary patches now track the game across the 1.26.x updates.

description.p: Internal refactor into a Python package, with fixes for the open bug reports:

ul.li: fixes a startup crash and a spurious Steam launch caused by Proton's VR client;
fixes the Flatpak failing to start under a pure Wayland session;
makes Microsoft sign-in reliable by ensuring the required Python dependency is present on every install.

description.p: Flatpak packaging, plus two Wayland input fixes:

ul.li: opt-in gamescope (BOL_GAMESCOPE=1) so the in-game mouse works on Wayland sessions, where XWayland otherwise drops it;
the opt-in winewayland backend now falls back to XWayland automatically when it cannot open a window, so the game always starts.

description.p: Fixes for the opt-in winewayland backend and safer prefix repair.
